title: <div><tt>+| **Lexical drift** | Language evolves; static lexicons will become ou
content:
+| **Lexical drift** | Language evolves; static lexicons will become outdated | Implement drift detection and periodic lexicon updates through community feedback【3†L165-L185】. |
+| **Cognitive overload** | Overly dense visualizations or multi‑layer outputs may overwhelm users | Provide adaptive visualization filters and entropy caps; allow users to toggle layers. |
+| **Semantic drift in generation** | Aggressive paraphrasing or rhetorical embellishment may alter truth | Enforce semantic similarity checks and NLI gates; limit rhetorical operations in sensitive domains. |
+| **Low‑resource languages** | Morphological/syntactic models may underperform on under‑represented languages | Integrate unsupervised or few‑shot learning techniques; solicit community corpora and incorporate morphological analyzers. |
+| **Ethical governance** | Decisions about harmful content may vary across cultures | Use participatory ethical councils; maintain audit logs for public gate decisions. |
+| **Automation brittleness** | Copilot may misinterpret open‑ended instructions | Provide clear, atomic tasks; label each work step and expected outputs precisely. |
